snapshot: Nikkei 225 night-session futures closed at 59,390 and TOPIX futures at 3,817. The Nikkei future still sits slightly below the prior cash close, so the open is not a one-way gap-up case.
Reuters expects a range-bound start, with the Nikkei seen between 59,200 and 59,700 as Friday profit-taking offsets peace-talk optimism.
U.S. equities closed at fresh highs again, with the S&P 500 at 7,041.28 and the Nasdaq at 24,102.70. Brent finished at $99.39 and WTI at $94.69.
USD/JPY is holding in the upper 158s. The worst of the 'bad yen' pressure seen near 160 has eased, though official rhetoric remains a live risk.

drivers.why_today: Japan is opening after a record-setting rally, so some giveback is natural, but hopes for progress in U.S.-Iran talks are still providing support. Reuters' Nikkei range is 59,200-59,700.

drivers.driver: Oil is still high, but FX is calmer than at the 160 line
drivers.why_today: Brent and WTI remain elevated, yet the panic tone has eased. USD/JPY is in the upper 158s rather than testing 160, which softens some of the import-cost pressure.

drivers.driver: After-close cancellations and earnings revisions are the cleanest stock-specific drivers
drivers.why_today: Bandai Namco, Oro, Datasection and Asahi Diamond all delivered easy-to-explain disclosures after the close, making this a better day for stock-picking than for broad-market chasing.

headline_inventory.headline: Nikkei 225 night-session futures closed at 59,390 and TOPIX futures at 3,817
Today's Tokyo outlook is for a range-bound market, with the Nikkei seen between 59,200 and 59,700
U.S. equities closed with fresh record highs in the S&P 500 and Nasdaq, while Brent finished at $99.39 and WTI at $94.69
Japan's finance minister said the government was watching markets with an extremely high sense of urgency as oil futures spill over into FX
Oro announced cancellation of shares equal to 2.19% of shares outstanding
Bandai Namco Holdings announced cancellation of 5 million treasury shares
Datasection revised up its FY March 2026 profit forecast
Harmonic Drive and other robot-related names were bid after news that NVIDIA is accelerating its AI robotics strategy
Asahi Diamond revised its full-year ordinary profit from a decline to a 10% increase

watchlist.name: Bandai Namco Holdings
watchlist.why_today: The company announced cancellation of 5 million treasury shares. It is a large, liquid name with one of the easiest supply-improvement stories to monitor into the open.
watchpoints: Can it stay firm after approaching the 4,000-yen area
Does the market reward the treasury cancellation cleanly even in a Friday tape
Does buying stay concentrated in the stock rather than in entertainment names broadly
falsifiers: The market treats the cancellation as largely priced in
Friday profit-taking fills most of the opening gap
watchlist.red_team: The catalyst is clear, but as a large-cap stock it can lose momentum quickly after the opening burst.

watchlist.name: Oro
watchlist.why_today: Oro announced cancellation of shares equal to 2.19% of shares outstanding. Coming after its March buyback, the supply story is still straightforward and visible.
watchpoints: Can it establish itself above the 2,000-yen area
Does the cancellation keep attracting buying after the opening print
Can it resist any weakness in smaller growth names
falsifiers: The market assumes the prior buyback already priced in most of the effect
Turnover dries up after the initial opening reaction
watchlist.red_team: The supply story is good, but smaller-cap follow-through can still become unstable quickly.

watchlist.name: Datasection
watchlist.why_today: The company lifted its FY March 2026 profit outlook again, taking the record-profit story one step further. The key issue is whether the prior-session reaction can extend.
watchpoints: Can it retake or exceed the prior session's high zone
Does the market continue to reward the guidance raise on a straightforward basis
Can the stock hold up if Growth sentiment softens
falsifiers: Investors question the quality of the profit improvement
The prior-day move turns into a sell-the-news reaction at the open
watchlist.red_team: Project delays remain in the background, so investors may scrutinize the quality of the uplift.

データセクが３日続伸、２６年３月期利益予想を上方修正

watchlist.name: Harmonic Drive Systems
watchlist.why_today: The stock surged on the NVIDIA-Cadence robotics AI angle, and the Nasdaq's 12-session winning streak still supports the broader AI-robotics theme into today's open.
watchpoints: Can it stay near the prior session's high zone after the open
Does the physical-AI theme continue to attract fresh money
Can the stock hold up even if the broader semiconductor group wobbles
falsifiers: The prior session's theme surge immediately cools
U.S. tech futures soften and pull semi-related names lower
watchlist.red_team: This is still a theme-led move, so sentiment can matter more than near-term earnings evidence.

deep_dive.title: Bandai Namco: the key question is whether the cancellation story can keep carrying after the opening gap

lines: The heart of the setup is not earnings or IP news but a simple and visible supply-improvement story through the cancellation of 5 million treasury shares.
Because the name is large and liquid, it is one of the easiest stock-specific catalysts to validate right at the open.
At 15:30 JST on April 16, the company disclosed the treasury-share cancellation, and the next-morning buyback roundup immediately highlighted it as one of the key after-close items.
With the broader market expected to range-trade rather than trend strongly, stock-specific supply improvement should stand out more clearly.
Can it stay firm after approaching the 4,000-yen area rather than fading after the opening burst.
Does turnover remain stock-specific enough to show that this is more than just a broader entertainment-sector move.
The market treats the cancellation as already priced in.
Friday profit-taking fills most of the opening gap and removes the relative-strength profile.
As a large-cap stock, it can lose momentum quickly once the first wave of buying is complete.
If the market slips back into index-led trading, the stock-specific catalyst can get buried.

no_trade_conditions: If the ceasefire story is reversed before the open and crude spikes sharply again
If USD/JPY accelerates back toward 160 and intervention fears plus import-cost pressure return at the same time
If Osaka night futures fail to hold near 54,040 and the gap-up premise breaks before cash trading begins
If San-A and Daiseki fail to hold their opening premiums and the market slips back into a simple index-only rebound
